1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.
8	
9	DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
10	- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
11	- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
12	- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output
13	
14	## Architecture
15	
16	This monorepo produces three artifacts:
17	- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
18	- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
19	- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site
20	
21	### Key Components
22	
23	- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
24	  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
25	  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
26	  - `main.go` - Application entry point
27	- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
28	- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`).
29	  All plugins are project-local: `ddx install <name>` writes only under
30	  `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and
31	  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Home-directory install paths
32	  (under `~/`) and the `ddx install --global` surface are retired.
33	  - `templates/` - Project templates
34	  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
35	  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
36	  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
37	  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
38	- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
39	- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts
40	
41	## Development Commands
42	
43	### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)
44	
45	```bash
46	# Build and test
47	make build          # Build for current platform
48	make test           # Run Go tests
49	make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
50	make fmt            # Format Go code

227-## Execution Evidence Convention
228-
229:[Omitted long matching line]
230-
231-## When filing beads

225	See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
226	
227	## Execution Evidence Convention
228	
229	When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.
230	
231	## When filing beads
232	
233	A bead's body is the entire prompt the executing sub-agent will see — there is no chat history, no operator hand-curation, no out-of-band context. Every bead must therefore satisfy the 8-criterion rubric documented in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` before it is filed or dispatched.
234	
235	In short:
236	
237	- **Title**: imperative, names subsystem + change.
238	- **Description**: PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE WITH `path/file.go:LINE` + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE.
239	- **AC**: numbered, each verifiable; at least one names a `Test*` function or `go test -run` filter; final two lines are `cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`.

input.old_string:
## Execution Evidence Convention

When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.
input.new_string:
## Execution Evidence Convention

`.ddx/executions/` is **per-machine working state** — like `node_modules`, it is gitignored and not tracked in the repository. The durable audit trail lives in `.ddx/attachments/` (the events stream produced by `ddx bead migrate`).

When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths do not survive between machines and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.

data.text:
Commit succeeded: 12,118 files changed (12,116 deletions of tracked execution files, plus the `.gitignore` and `CLAUDE.md` edits). All ACs are satisfied:

1. **AC1**: Lines 438-445 (the un-ignore allow-list) are gone from `.gitignore`.
2. **AC2**: `.ddx/executions/` is now an explicit ignore in `.gitignore`.
3. **AC3**: `git ls-files .ddx/executions/` returns 0 matches.
4. **AC4**: Future attempt dirs will be untracked (gitignore now covers the whole subtree).
5. **AC5**: `CLAUDE.md` documents `.ddx/executions/` as per-machine working state with `.ddx/attachments/` as the durable audit.
6. **AC6**: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` — all green.
7. **AC7**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
